question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 lowers MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R3↔R4 link) from 1514 to
1400 while R4 stays at default 1514. OSPF DBD packets sized for the
larger MTU cannot be reassembled across the link; OSPF neighbor stalls
in EXSTART. Because R3 is the only OSPF transit between R2 and R4,
the iBGP session over loopbacks eventually drops as well (route to
192.0.2.4 disappears once the LSDB de-synchronizes).
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R3, the MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R3<->R4 link) was lowered to 1400 while R4 stays at 1514.
The two ends disagree on the link MTU, so the oversize OSPF database-description packets cannot be exchanged across the asymmetric link and the OSPF adjacency cannot complete. With OSPF unable to synchronize its database, the route to R4's loopback (192.0.2.4) is never installed, so the loopback-based iBGP session has no underlying reachability and cannot stay established, and prefixes stop propagating between R2 and R4.
Fix: remove the MTU override on R3's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (restore 1514) so both ends agree on the MTU and OSPF can synchronize again.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.2.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R2, R4 (2 instances):
- R2: Changed state to NSR-Ready
- R4: Changed state to NSR-Ready

revert.commands:
configure terminal
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 no mtu

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:13:54.965 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Down - BGP Notification sent, hold time expired (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:13:55.231 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.2 Down - BGP Notification sent, hold time expired (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:13:54.968 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:13:55.232 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
